heading: Capital grants for class II and class III railroads
label: 22301 Capital grants for class II and class III railroads

children.label: i
children.text: rehabilitate, preserve, or improve railroad track (including roadbed, bridges, and related track structures) used primarily for freight transportation;

children.label: ii
children.text: facilitate the continued or greater use of railroad transportation for freight shipments; and

children.label: iii
children.text: reduce the use of less fuel efficient modes of transportation in the transportation of such shipments; or

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrate innovative technologies and advanced research and development that increase fuel economy, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and lower the costs of operation.

children.label: A
children.text: directly to the class II or class III railroad; or

children.label: B
children.text: with the concurrence of the class II or class III railroad, to a State or local government.

children.heading: .—State cooperation
children.label: 3 .—State cooperation
children.text: Class II and class III railroad applicants for a grant under this chapter are encouraged to utilize the expertise and assistance of State transportation agencies in applying for and administering such grants. State transportation agencies are encouraged to provide such expertise and assistance to such railroads.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 4 .—Regulations
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall issue final regulations to implement the program under this section.October 1, 2008

children.heading: .—Maximum Federal Share
children.label: b .—Maximum Federal Share
children.text: The maximum Federal share for carrying out a project under this section shall be 80 percent of the project cost. The non-Federal share may be provided by any non-Federal source in cash, equipment, or supplies. Other in-kind contributions may be approved by the Secretary on a case-by-case basis consistent with this chapter.

children.heading: .—Use of Funds
children.label: c .—Use of Funds
children.text: Grants provided under this section shall be used to implement track capital projects as soon as possible. In no event shall grant funds be contractually obligated for a project later than the end of the third Federal fiscal year following the year in which the grant was awarded. Any funds not so obligated by the end of such fiscal year shall be returned to the Secretary for reallocation.

children.heading: .—Employee Protection
children.label: d .—Employee Protection
children.text: The Secretary shall require as a condition of any grant made under this section that the recipient railroad provide a fair arrangement at least as protective of the interests of employees who are affected by the project to be funded with the grant as the terms imposed under section 11326(a), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Prevailing wages
children.label: 1 .—Prevailing wages
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that laborers and mechanics employed by contractors and subcontractors in construction work financed by a grant made under this section will be paid wages not less than those prevailing on similar construction in the locality, as determined by the Secretary of Labor under subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40 (commonly known as the “Davis-Bacon Act”). The Secretary shall make a grant under this section only after being assured that required labor standards will be maintained on the construction work.

children.heading: .—Wage rates
children.label: 2 .—Wage rates
children.text: Wage rates in a collective bargaining agreement negotiated under the Railway Labor Act ( et seq.) are deemed for purposes of this subsection to comply with the subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40.45 U.S.C. 1511So in original. The word “the” probably should not appear.1

children.heading: .—Study
children.label: f .—Study
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a study of the projects carried out with grant assistance under this section to determine the extent to which the program helps promote a reduction in fuel use associated with the transportation of freight and demonstrates innovative technologies that increase fuel economy, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and lower the costs of operation. Not later than , the Secretary shall submit a report to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate on the study, including any recommendations the Secretary considers appropriate regarding the program.March 31, 2009

children.heading: .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.label: g .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2011 for carrying out this section.
